notes: Tier 1: Consistent Top-Rated or Product of the Year winners across 2023-2025.
Tier 2: Approved/certified products with strong but less consistent results.
Tier 3: All other VT vendors — low-weight, noisy, or untested by independent labs.
VT engine names may differ slightly from vendor marketing names (e.g. 'ESET-NOD32' vs 'ESET').

tier1.description: Top-Rated / Product of the Year winners — highest detection rates, lowest false positives, consistent across 3 years of independent testing.

evidence.Bitdefender: Outstanding Product 2023, Top-Rated 2024 & 2025, lowest avg compromised systems (0.5) across 3 years of business tests
evidence.ESET: Product of the Year 2024, Advanced+ in all 7 tests in 2025, Top-Rated 2023 & 2025
evidence.Kaspersky: Top-Rated 2024 & 2025, lowest false positive count (5) in 2024, Advanced+ in malware protection 2025
evidence.Avast: Top-Rated 2024 & 2025, Advanced+ in 6/7 tests in 2025, Gold award for overall performance
evidence.AVG: Top-Rated 2024 & 2025, Advanced+ in 6/7 tests in 2025, Gold award for overall performance
evidence.G Data: Top-Rated 2024 & 2025, consistently Advanced+ in malware protection
evidence.CrowdStrike: Certified in AV-Comparatives Enterprise Business Security Test 2024, industry-standard EDR/XDR

tier2.description: Approved/certified products — solid detection rates, passed independent testing, but not consistent Top-Rated winners.

evidence.Microsoft: Advanced+ in March 2026 malware protection test, approved enterprise product, widely deployed
evidence.Sophos: Approved enterprise product, long-standing independent test participant
evidence.Trend Micro: Certified in enterprise tests 2024, though lower stalkerware detection (59%) in EFF/AVC 2025 test
evidence.Malwarebytes: 100% stalkerware detection in EFF/AVC 2025 test, approved consumer product
evidence.VIPRE: Advanced+ in March 2025 malware protection test, 98.7% detection rate, 99.93% overall protection
evidence.Elastic: Approved enterprise product in 2024 business security test

tier3.description: All other vendors not in Tier 1 or Tier 2. Still count as signal but with low weight. Includes noisy engines, regional vendors, and those without independent test track records.
tier3.note: Any vendor not listed in tier1 or tier2 defaults to tier3 automatically in the scoring logic.

tag_weights._note: Weights for VT/OTX tags on network IOCs (IPs, domains). Score is summed then capped at tag_cap.

medium._comment: Suspicious infrastructure — not always malicious but worth escalating

low._comment: Weak signal on its own — only meaningful combined with other tags

vt_engine_name_aliases.note: VirusTotal engine names sometimes differ from vendor marketing names. Map them here.
